content:
# Tiered Knowledge: MCP Tools + CLI Commands (Issue #24)

## Implementation Summary

Added the user-facing control plane for tiered knowledge lifecycle management:

### MCP Tools (3 new, total 21)
- `corvia_pin` (Tier 1): Pin entry by ID with agent audit trail. Requires agent_id via param or _meta.agent_id.
- `corvia_unpin` (Tier 2/LowRisk): Unpin with confirmation. Makes entry eligible for GC demotion again.
- `corvia_inspect` (Tier 1): Full lifecycle metadata — tier, retention_score, memory_type, access history, pin state, inbound edges.

### CLI Commands (6 new)
- `corvia gc status/run/history`: Operator observability for knowledge GC
- `corvia pin/unpin/inspect`: Entry lifecycle management

### Architecture Decisions
- Pin is Tier 1 (additive/protective, no confirmation needed). Unpin is Tier 2 (removes safety guardrail).
- CLI `unpin` requires `--yes` flag for consistency with MCP confirmation.
- `GcKnowledgeHistory` is an in-memory ring buffer (50 entries) — ephemeral, lost on restart.
- Pin/unpin use `update_entry_metadata` (whole-entry write). Known RMW race with concurrent access tracking — matches existing GC pattern, deferred to buffered-write refactor (#12).
- Pin is idempotent: re-pinning overwrites previous pin info (last pinner wins).

### Known Limitations (deferred)
- `corvia gc run` CLI triggers knowledge GC; MCP `corvia_gc_run` triggers staging GC — naming collision to resolve in follow-up.
- Periodic background GC cycles are not recorded in `GcKnowledgeHistory` — only manual triggers via REST.
- Pin/unpin kernel ops downcast to LiteStore; PostgresStore not yet supported.
- REST endpoints have no authentication gate (local-first assumption).

PR: chunzhe10/corvia#51
